hook: AI gave you a 400-word essay when you asked for a quick answer. That's not intelligence. That's insecurity.
caption:
AI gave you a 400-word essay when you asked for a quick answer. That's not intelligence. That's insecurity.

Here's what's actually happening:

AI defaults to *comprehensive* because it doesn't know if you're a beginner who needs hand-holding or an expert who wants one sentence. So it writes for both. And helps neither.

The dirty secret of AI responses:

Every hedge. Every "it's important to note." Every "there are several factors to consider" — that's the AI covering its bases because it has zero idea how your brain works.

It doesn't know you've been in this industry for 11 years.
It doesn't know you hate bullet points.
It doesn't know you read the conclusion first.
It doesn't know you want the answer, not the explanation.

So it gives you the explanation anyway. Every. Single. Time.

This is the gap bootfile.ai was built for.

Your BootFile tells AI exactly how you process information — your depth preference, your tolerance for caveats, whether you think in systems or in stories. So the first response actually fits how you think.

Not the third response. Not after you've typed "shorter" twice.

The first one.

Stop training AI from scratch every conversation. Let it already know you.
